suggestions.topic: PTC fix decision outcome and DevNet 1 timeline
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #176 deferred final PTC fix decision to March 24 ACDT. This is the only hard fork requirement for DevNet 1, making it a critical blocking issue that needs status confirmation.

ACDC #176 set mid-April target for DevNet 0 before Interop. With DevNet 0 still unstable (Lighthouse exits bug, Prysm FCU issues, Nimbus/Teku/Grandine not operational), a comprehensive readiness assessment is needed.

ACDC #176 identified that 4788 will have gaps for empty payloads under ePBS. Stokes was tasked with contacting Lido, Rocketpool, and EigenLayer to assess impact and proof complexity. This affects critical protocol dependencies.

suggestions.topic: SSZ Engine API transport and redesign scope
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #176 showed significant blob performance gains with SSZ transport (PR 764) but revealed tension between shipping immediately vs full Engine API redesign. ELs want semantic fixes to FCU bundling and validity checks. Cross-team coordination needed.

suggestions.topic: BAL DevNet 2 SFI discussion results from ACDE
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #176 scheduled BAL DevNet 2 SFI discussion for ACDE March 26. Results should be reported back to understand BAL readiness timeline and any blockers.

suggestions.topic: Partial slot proofs implementation status (Teku, Lodestar, Nimbus)
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #173 established that ePBS cannot ship without partial slot proofs in all clients. Lighthouse and Prism had implementations in January, but Teku, Lodestar, and Nimbus completion status remains unconfirmed.

suggestions.topic: Hegotá non-headliner proposals (EIP-8148 custom sweep threshold)
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #176 mentioned non-headliner proposal window opens after EL headliner decision. EIP-8148 (custom sweep threshold for 0x02 validators) was introduced. Time to review initial proposals.

ACDC #174 showed Lighthouse nearly complete, Prism in review, Lodestar debugging. Since this can deploy pre-Amsterdam with backwards compatibility, checking final status and deployment timeline is informational but non-blocking.
